revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:判事]]
'''Hanji''' (判事, Kotowaru Tsukasa) refers to an official position within the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, specifically under the {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|qid=Q6867320|lt=Ministry of Justice|1=Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} outside the {{ill|Four-Tier System of Officials|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-Tier System of Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan|qq=}} and was responsible for judicial hearings and rulings. Hanji positions also existed in the {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|qid=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}}.
== Overview == Hanji presided over judicial hearings alongside the {{ill|Gyōbukyō|ja|刑部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōbukyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 刑部省|1=Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6867320}} (Minister of Justice), reviewing records of interrogations, known as {{ill|Kukujō|ja|鞫状|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukujō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, submitted by the {{ill|Tōkibe|ja|解部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkibe|1=Tōkibe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17229315}} (investigators). They determined a11licable laws from the {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|qid=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō|1=Traditional Chinese law}} codes and formulated judgments. The Ranks and duties of Hanji were as follows: - **Daihanji** (大判事): 2 positions, equivalent to {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}. - **Chūhanji** (中判事): 4 positions, equivalent to {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正六位下|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}}. - **Shōhanji** (少判事): 4 positions, equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従六位下|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}}.<ref>Articles 30, 10, 12, and 13 of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō|qq=}} and {{ill|Kan-i-ryō|ja|官位令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-i-ryō|1=Official Rank Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97341948}}.</ref>
In the Dazaifu: - **Daihanji**: 1 position, equivalent to {{ill|Junior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従六位下|1=Junior Sixth Rank|lt=Junior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従六位}}. - **Shōhanji**: 1 position, equivalent to {{ill|Senior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade|ja|正七位上|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}.<ref>Articles 69, 13, and 14 of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō|qq=}} and {{ill|Kan-i-ryō|ja|官位令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-i-ryō|1=Official Rank Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97341948}}.</ref>
Many Hanji were a11ointed from the {{ill|Myōbōdō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōbōdō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512427}} (legal scholars), though some were selected from {{ill|Monjō Tokugyōsei|ja|文章得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Tokugyōsei|1=monjoutokugoushou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97378716}} (graduates specializing in literature).<ref>''Iwanami Japanese Historical Dictionary'', p. 953.</ref>
Before the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大宝律令|1=Taihō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1059892}}, evidence from the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} suggests that Hanji existed in the judicial system of the Ministry of Justice. For instance, the 26th scroll of the Nihon Shoki describes: {{quotation|On a later day, Prince Arima conspired with a Hanji (Kotowaru Tsukasa) in rebellion.}}<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Scroll 26, Emperor Saimei, November of the 4th year.</ref> This implies the existence of Hanji alongside the judicial offices established during the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reforms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大化の改新|1=Taika Reform|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q570140}}.
According to Scroll 30 of the Nihon Shoki, in the 3rd year of Emperor Jitō's reign (689), several individuals were a11ointed as Hanji, including {{ill|Takeda no Ō|ja|竹田王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda no Ō|1=Takeda no Ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20041199}}, {{ill|Haji no Nemaro|ja|土師根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji no Nemaro|1=Haji no Nemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q92924443}}, {{ill|Ōyake no Maro|ja|大宅麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyake no Maro|1=Ōyake no Maro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q92670436}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|qid=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}}, {{ill|Tōma no Sakurai|ja|当麻桜井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōma no Sakurai|1=Taima no Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30935802}}, {{ill|Hozumi no Yamamori|ja|穂積山守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi no Yamamori|1=Hozumi no Yamamori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22129418}}, {{ill|Nakatomi no Omimaro|ja|中臣意美麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatomi no Omimaro|1=Nakatomi no Omimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11366913}}, {{ill|Kose no Tamikusu|ja|巨勢多益須|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Tamikusu|1=Kose no Tayeisu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30934407}}, and {{ill|Ōgami no Yasumaro|ja|大神安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgami no Yasumaro|1=Omiwa no Yasumaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30933815}}.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Jitō, 3rd year, February 26th.</ref>
By the mid-Heian period, the Hanji position became hereditary within the {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11363164}} and {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10928477}}.<ref>''Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary,'' p. 787.</ref> During the 8th year of {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|qid=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} (896), the number of Hanji under the Ritsuryō system was reduced.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * ''Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary,'' p. 787, edited by {{ill|Mitsuhisa Takayanagi|ja|高柳光寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsuhisa Takayanagi|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11670539}} and {{ill|Rizō Takeuchi|en|Rizō Takeuchi|qid=Q4262779|lt=Rizō Takeuchi|1=Rizō Takeuchi}}, Kadokawa Shoten, 1966. * ''Iwanami Japanese Historical Dictionary,'' p. 953, supervised by {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11549910}}, Iwanami Shoten, 1999. * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes IV and V), Iwanami Bunko, 1995. * ''Complete Modern Translation of the Nihon Shoki'' (Volume 2), translated by {{ill|Takeshi Ujita|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeshi Ujita|1=Ujitani Mitsuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449517}}, Kodansha Academic Bunko, 1988.
{{translated page|ja|判事|version=102284562|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:判事]]
